pages.text:
Gloucester–Hereford canal, lots of Navvies buried in the churchyard.
Ashperton tunnel unstable.
    We left early to get back to Sunday church at St. G.  Simon lost van key.

__Wednesday July 6th__
9.15. Simon, Finder & Eve drove to Charles' & Jenny's new house in Oxford, Frenchay Rd off Woodstock Rd.  A week ago he beat the nasty woman next door in planning permission for a window.  House full of workmen working on shell.  Rooms clear.  V. exciting.                                          Haypr
On to deliver lights Watlington & [?send] Hinley.
On to Peter & Nana's house at Finsbury Pk.
Our last visit before they move after July 19th.
We slept in van & had breakfast with Nana & Thomas.  Peter in Wales & SW with Conrad, Eleni & [?Satter]-in-law Chhatrapati.  Nana was doing a dance exhibition which we had to miss.  After watching a bit of Murray at Wimbledon we went to the Merchant Taylors, gorgeous, & had a BBQ in courtyard put on by [illegible].  We (Chad) had invited 3 designers.

We tried to speak to as many designers as possible.  I sat next to a very nice Irishman who did sea diving & had 3 daughters & a (small) salvage ~~card~~ company.  He did the joinery on the QE2.

__Thursday__
    It's been so hot today & yesterday we've had to keep the chocolate in the fridge.
We went to the end of year London Show.
Jason (the designer's husband) gave us tickets.  It was WOW!
A man on [?Bham] stand had a stool made of felt – actually plastic bottle felt.  Compacted.  Simon liked the texture & wants to make lights out of it.
Another man wants to cut down on carbon footprint of light-making.  He puts maple chips
                                                              ← mushroom
    maple                                                         mould
                                                                 spores.
in a mould &
Ingots in a mould                     + heat 200
like dry rot.                              for 20 min.

the mould grows & grows into the shape of a tho light (shade). or whatever. To kill the mould you heat it when you get it out of the mould. It's water & fire resistant. (A company in the US is using huge warehouses to make packaging materials of Ikea).
Another man had made an iphone speaker out of bamboo. Magnifying the sound without electricity.
Another student with shakey hands had a hot water boiling water dispenser so old people didn't have to lift a kettle.
    We drove on & found a carpark next to St. Mary's Headley & Box Hill, a well-kept church, for lunch.
    On to see Arathna & Godfrey. Eng 12. & Evans 10, spent all our visit playing with Tinder.

captions:
PRAYER OF ST RICHARD OF CHICHESTER
Thanks be to thee, my Lord Jesus Christ,
For all the benefits which Thou hast given me,
For all the pains and insults
Which Thou hast borne for me,
O most merciful Redeemer,
Friend and Brother,
May I know Thee more clearly,
Love Thee more dearly
And follow Thee more nearly.
CHICHESTER CATHEDRAL
pages.text:
We are at a farm above Steyning. We walked 1½ miles to the Chequers where we had good ham & chips but no pudding.
    Tinder has been very good.
Friday
    We visited Simon & Claudia Ogden for elevenses. They were very welcoming. Then we joined the Gravestocks who gave us a delicious lunch. We went for a walk through a park & watched water skiing & saw the sea.
    At △ we left for our campsite The Ship & Anchor at Littlehampton ***
There's a bike path to Arundel, but it's very overgrown.

captions: Arundel R.C. Cathedral. Gate of mercy. Nice church.
pages.text:
Saturday.
We set off, cycling along the raised embankment next to the R. Arun. Hardgoing because of June grasses & thistles. We could see Arundel Castle in
[photo of Arundel Cathedral]
Arundel R.C. Cathedral. Gate of mercy.
Nice church.
Philip Howard
this dog.

captions: The Chagall window (detail)- see 13 Chichester Cathedral
pages.text:
the distance which I first mistook for another cement works. It got nearer & nearer. The wind was pushing us.
  We had a coffee & I bought lavender for Venetia whom we've visiting tomorrow in her new house. We also bought pastries for supper. etc
  We caught a train back to Ford Station along the road to the campsite pub for lund which we had booked for 12.30. Good [?Affo] steak & kidney pie.
  After a nap we drove to Chichester & looked at the shops & went to Evensong. Who Should turn up but the Roses! We stopped at Bognor Regis (George V bugger Bognor!) on the way back to the campsite for supper in the van.
  5 0p each tokens for showers. Good Showers.
[photo: The Chagall window (detail)- see 13 Chichester Cathedral]
